title: Harness quality check advisory report
summary: Advisory-only audit orchestration smoke: existing Harness Hub checks are composed into a local HTML report without remote side effects.

intent.primaryQuestion: Can the harness-quality-check skill compose existing checks into an advisory HTML report?
intent.decision: Review advisory findings and choose follow-up work; no enforcement happens in this report.
successCriteria: Commands and evidence coverage are visible.
Findings, next actions, and coverage gaps are separated.
Advisory-only and no-remote-side-effect boundaries are visible.

sections.title: Scope and boundary
sections.content:
- Scope: Harness Hub source checkout and target repository.
- Advisory-only: findings are triage guidance, not enforcement.
- No remote writes, schedules, hooks, commits, pushes, or enforcement changes were performed.
- No remote side effects: this smoke covers a local HTML report only.

sections.title: Command evidence coverage
sections.summary: Fixture summaries stand in for the commands the skill should compose during a live audit.

rows.evidence: CLI health and managed component freshness are represented.
Agent readiness, harness gaps, automation risk, and verification readiness are summarized.
rows.coverage: Target repository readiness and harness shape.

rows.evidence: Required harness files and state templates are represented.
Skill metadata, trigger hygiene, source records, and routing-sensitive descriptions are summarized.

rows.finding: Target task state lacks acceptance criteria or validation commands.
A routing-sensitive description changed without matching fixture coverage.
rows.next: Add positive and boundary routing cases.

rows.finding: Quality snapshot exists but does not name the follow-up owner.
rows.next: Record an owner or defer explicitly.

rows.finding: This report is advisory-only and did not alter hooks, remotes, credentials, commits, pushes, or schedules.
rows.next: Keep enforcement separate from the advisory report.

sections.title: Coverage gaps
sections.content:
- This fixture uses simulated command summaries, so it proves report orchestration rather than live target correctness.
- It does not prove downstream quality improvement, reviewer behavior, or defect reduction.
- Browser validation is skipped in the smoke test; artifact structure is still validated deterministically.

sections.title: Next actions
items: Review high and medium advisory findings.
Decide whether to run live target commands for freshness evidence.
Keep enforcement, hooks, schedules, commits, pushes, and remote writes outside this advisory skill.

evidence.label: Self-check summary
evidence.value: Simulated local CLI and managed component health summary.

evidence.label: Agent readiness and harness analysis summary
evidence.value: Simulated readiness summary with advisory gaps.

evidence.label: Harness validation summary
evidence.value: Simulated required-file and state-template validation summary.

evidence.label: Skill quality inventory summary
evidence.value: Simulated skill metadata and routing hygiene summary.

evidence.label: Harness quality check skill body
evidence.value: The skill defines advisory HTML reporting boundaries.

verification.label: Fixture generation
verification.detail: create-interaction.mjs can render this advisory report fixture.

verification.label: HTML validation
verification.detail: validate-interaction.mjs accepts the generated HTML with skip-browser in the smoke test.

nextActions: Review high and medium advisory findings.
Run live target commands before treating findings as current.
Keep enforcement separate from advisory reporting.
